programme.title: Amor comprado

programme.title: Season 1
programme.desc: The pilot outline for "Amores Verdaderos" "Amores Verdaderos pilot introduces Victoria, a successful woman engaged to Armando. She meets José Ángel, a humble gardener, leading to unexpected connections and family conflicts."

programme.title: Telegrama
programme.desc: Nelson sale de la operation pero pueden amputar una pierna. Adriana recibe telegrama pidiéndole 5M para darle info sobre su hija. Nelson le dice a Kendra quien es la hija de Adriana.

programme.title: Resultados de ADN
programme.desc: Aníbal le hace una oferta a Nelson. Nelson le cuenta a Kendra sobre la carta de candelaria. Gilda le confiesa a Beatriz sobre la enfermedad de Paula. Santino renuncia trabajar por Aníbal. ADN confirma q Mia es hija de Nelson.

programme.title: Adriana embarazada
programme.desc: Nelson dice a Guzmán q apoyara a el con Nikki. Aníbal hace q cancelen la producción de AV. Adriana no le dice a Carlos q esperan bebe. Roy renuncia porq Liliana le dice q ama a Guzmán. Victoria regresa y le da una gran noticia a Arriaga.

programme.title: De Balvanera a González
programme.desc: Aníbal le regala el vivero y tarjetas de crédito a Liliana. Victoria visita a Nelson. Ya están divorciados. Beatriz organiza una fiesta para Paula. Adriana y Victoria felices de esperar bebés.

programme.title: Fiesta de Paula
programme.desc: Polita escucha q Victoria y Arriaga esperan bebe y espera a Liliana para contarle. Festejan a Paula pero lamentablemente ella no resiste. Aníbal y candelaria se casaron. Adriana y Beatriz viven en el condo de Victoria.

programme.title: Al borde de la muerte
programme.desc: Liliana insulta a su padre. Santino regresa de Brasil. Santino, Arriaga y Guzmán crean una agencia de seguridad. Espanto ataca a Liliana. Vicente la está operando. Adriana comienza su parto.

programme.title: Reencuentro de amor
programme.desc: Vicente y Adriana se encuentran y se cuentan las fechorías de Aníbal. Nelson pide perdón a Guzmán. Adriana le reclama a Aníbal. Nikki le avisa a Roy sobre Lili. Carlos regresa y Adriana le miente.

programme.title: Verdadera hija
programme.desc: Liliana despierta y quiere casarse con Guzmán. Kendra trata de seducir a Aníbal. Candelaria le dice a Adriana toda la verdad.

programme.title: A trabajar todos
programme.desc: A Mexican television series that centers on the López household.

programme.title: La venta del edificio
programme.desc: A Mexican television series that centers on the López household.

programme.title: Ahí va el agua
programme.desc: A Mexican television series that centers on the López household.

programme.title: Tome su turno
programme.desc: A Mexican television series that centers on the López household.

programme.title: Sostengo el Pi
programme.desc: María de todos los Ángeles, ve atentamente un programa de televisión en el que el maestro de yoga da tres consejos básicos del feng shui para atraer la fortuna y el amor, esto provoca que María inmediatamente se ponga a mover muebles y a tirar cosas, al ver esto Carmelito pone el grito en el cielo, hasta que su nieta lo convence de que con esto sencillos cambios podrán atraer la fortuna y el dinero. Delfino y Betsabé llegan a casa de Doña Lucha a cobrar el dinero de una tanda que les debe de tiempo atrás, ésta que también ha visto el programa, y no tiene para pagarles, usa los argumentos de maestro Chuy y los convence de que se inscriban en un curso de feng shui que va a impartir, con lo cual aprovecha para ponerlos a mover los muebles de su casa, hasta que ya cansados deciden abandonar el curso. Como segundo paso Doña Lucha decide escombrar la casa y comienza a tirar cosas de Rosa Aurora, Albertano y del Chino, pero el mono de coco que ella trajo de Acapulco lo vuelve a colocar en el mismo lugar. La euforia por limpiar lleva a las dos familias hasta la azotea en la que se desata un conflicto por el espacio y por el respeto a sus pertenencias.

programme.title: Ofertín y los vinagres balsámicos
programme.desc: Albertano sale a la calle a buscar trabajo para que El Chino pueda ir a una excursión a los prismas basalticos, María se encuentra abatida ya que su abuelo quiere desaparecer la botarga de Ofertín quien baila junto a ella mientras anuncia sus ofertas, pues nadie se quiere meter dentro de esta, Albertano se ofrece gustoso y María le paga una semana por adelantado, con este dinero El Chino podría ir a su excursión. A la mañana siguiente, Albertano descubre que Doña Lucha ha tirado a la basura la botarga de Ofertín por error. Don Carmelo amenaza con demandarlos pero Rosa Aurora decide tomar el lugar del peluche y baila seductoramente lo que causa revuelo en la colonia e incrementa las ganancias de la tienda.

programme.title: Haciéndole al muertito
programme.desc: El banco exige los pagos correspondientes, así que Ludovico finge su muerte para cancelar la deuda.

programme.title: Regalo de aniversario
programme.desc: Ludovico olvida su aniversario y no tiene cómo pagar un regalo. Luego él y Flavio quieren poner borracho a Don Camerino para aumentarles el salario y así comprar el regalo. Pero Don Camerino termina sobrio mientras ellos si se emborrachan. Como regalo se tatúan el nombre de sus esposas, aunque por error tatuándose Ludovico el nombre de Martina, y Flavio el nombre de Federica.

programme.title: Las pantuflas de Luis
programme.desc: Luis y Pedro hacen una apuesta para saber si se dice pantuflas o pantunflas y le preguntarán a todos los vecinos. Lorena sospecha que Benito está molesto por su nuevo galán.

programme.title: Segundo aire
programme.desc: Germán descubre que en el edificio hay una plaga de cucarachas, por lo que Magdalena le ordena que haga una inspección en su departamento.

programme.title: Número equivocado
programme.desc: Alguien grafiteó la fachada del edificio. Para retirarlo, Germán cambia el número de la entrada. ¿Recibirá Luis el paquete que espera? ¿Acudirán a pintar el departamento de Lorena?

programme.title: Magdalena y la tercera edad
programme.desc: Magdalena descubre que las personas de tercera edad recibirán tarjetas de ayuda. La familia López Pérez intenta obtener el beneficio. A Luis le molesta que Benito tenga un auto nuevo.

programme.title: Cásate conmigo
programme.desc: Comienza una guerra tras la pelea entre Silvia y Luis, sin embargo él le pide matrimonio. Lorena le pide a Germán que arregle una mesa.

programme.title: La boda
programme.desc: Luis y Silvia anuncian su boda y los vecinos los convencerán para que se casen en el edificio y ayudarán a los preparativos.

programme.title: Vecinos ruidosos
programme.desc: Los vecinos se quejan de los ruidos que vienen del departamento de Luis y Silvita, Germán intenta reparar la tubería de Lorena y los López Pérez sufren de comezón.

programme.title: Frankie regresa
programme.desc: Frankie regresa a vivir al edificio, con Lorena y con Benito, pero con la condición de que encuentre trabajo. Pero, en vez de eso, escribirá una nueva película.

programme.title: La llegada de Vanesa
programme.desc: Vanesa vuelve al edificio para visitar a los Vecinos y ponerse al corriente. Benito invita a su novia a "ver pelis" en el departamento, al enterarse de que sus papás saldrán.

programme.title: La cocina de Silvita
programme.desc: Como Luis le ha preparado el desayuno, Silvita se encargará de prepararle a su esposo un platillo espectacular.

programme.title: Vecinos
programme.desc: In a neighborhood in Mexico City, the neighbors have problems and silly misadventures with one another.

programme.title: La cosas de Don Roque
programme.desc: Germán le entrega a Vanessa cosas que le dejó don Roque, por lo que ambos lo recuerdan y homenajean. Lorena aprecia a Soledad, la nueva amiga de Benito.

programme.title: El tamalero
programme.desc: Como el tamalero no deja dormir a Luis, este le pedirá que le baje a su bocina. Ve cuántos enemigos se echará encima, por dejar sin trabajo al tamalero.

programme.title: Los paramédicos
programme.desc: Por pedirle a Morris que ponga en su tarea que son paramédicos, Rocko y Ale deben ir a la escuela a dar un curso de primeros auxilios. Llegan nuevos vecinos al departamento 301.

programme.title: Vecinos
programme.desc: In a neighborhood in Mexico City, the neighbors have problems and silly misadventures with one another.

programme.title: Los ex
programme.desc: Luis le pide a Silvita que deje de hablar con su ex, pues se siente incómodo, sin sospechar que Maru, ex de Luis, llegará a la puerta de su casa. Además, la obra de arte de Jorjais.

programme.title: El cumpleaños de Germán
programme.desc: Todos se olvidan del cumpleaños de Germán, a pesar de estar invitados a la fiesta. Lorena y Frankie le dicen a Benito que volverá a los castings.

programme.title: La amiga incómoda
programme.desc: La amiga de Silvita se queda en el departamento de los recién casados, ya que se ha quedado sin casa. Además, Magda trata de convencer a los vecinos para que acepten poner un anuncio en la fachada del edificio.

programme.title: Sortilegio
programme.desc: Alejandro returns home after a terrible accident to discover that he is married to Maria Jose, a poor, innocent girl who was deceived by Alejandro's brother Bruno. Bruno used Alejandro's name to marry Maria Jose, soon after he sent to kill his brother in hopes that Maria Jose would inherit Alejandro's fortune. But when Maria Jose arrives at the mansion she realizes that Bruno lied to her and used her. She is also scared when Alejandro is found alive and returns home. Doctors tell Alejandro that he suffers from memory loss but he is convinced he never met Maria Jose before and goes in search of the truth, but during this time and Maria Jose actually fall in love, while Bruno and Maura (Alejandro's ex-girlfriend) plot to break them up. Meanwhile, the brothers' mother Victoria has fallen in love with Fernando--Alejandro's best friend, so closer to his age than hers.

programme.title: Bitten

programme.title: MeatEater - Bear With Seal: Alaska Bear Hunt Part 2
programme.desc: Steven Rinella will take Navy SEAL Rorke Denver on his first hunt deep into the Alaskan backcountry to search for black bears in the mighty Alaska Range. In the remote wilderness, two friends will look into the differences and similarities between the hunter and the warrior. The bears are out but Rorke and Steve will have to work hard to put a tag on one.

programme.title: MeatEater - Yukon Giants: Northern Alaska Moose Part 1
programme.desc: Dropped off by bushplane on a remote lakeside in the wilderness of Alaska's Brooks Range, Steven Rinella and wildlife biologist Brandt Meixell have ten days of hunting before the plane returns. Big-antlered Yukon moose are few and far between but hard work pays off for hunters who stick with it until the bitter end.

programme.title: MeatEater - Yukon Giants: Northern Alaska Moose Part 2
programme.desc: Dropped off by bushplane on a remote lakeside in the wilderness of Alaskas Brooks Range.

programme.title: MeatEater - Deer the Hard Way: Prince of Wales Island (Joe Rogan and Bryan Callen)
programme.desc: Steven Rinella has hunted this area before, but he feels he's just scratched the surface exploring the surrounding country. The top of the mountain is always calling Steve's name, and for this serious Alaskan backpack hunt he's bringing along comedians Joe Rogan and Bryan Callen. Cold, wet weather and tough hunting for wily Sitka deer make this a southeast Alaskan adventure these hunters will never forget.

programme.title: MeatEater - Burger Extravaganza Cooking Special
programme.desc: As the prime cuts are often the first to go, most families are left with a freezer brimming with ground wild game meat. Pulled from the pages of his forthcoming Wild Game Cookbook, Steve shares a variety of recipes from burger to meatloaf and more ‚Äì designed to whittle down this supply and jazz up your ground meat game.

programme.title: MeatEater - Welcome to the Jungle: Adventures in Bolivia Part 1
programme.desc: Steve is fascinated by indigenous people who live in the wilds South America. In this three-part series, he travels deep into the Amazon jungle to hunt with and learn from the locals there. Not only will this be one heck of a hunting adventure, it will be a mind-opening, life-changing event. From bowfishing to hunting in the jungle at night, you never know what the evening's menu will bring.

programme.title: MeatEater - Welcome to the Jungle: Adventures in Bolivia Part 2
programme.desc: Steve is fascinated by indigenous people who live in the wilds South America.

programme.title: MeatEater - Welcome to the Jungle: Adventures in Bolivia Part 3
programme.desc: Steve is fascinated by indigenous people who live in the wilds South America.

programme.title: MeatEater - About Time: Helen and Brittany Hunt Elk in Montana
programme.desc: Brittany and Helen have put more work in on MeatEater than just about anyone else, but have yet to hunt.

programme.title: MeatEater - Sky Island Solitaire: Backpack Hunting Coues Deer in Arizona
programme.desc: Steve returns to southern Arizona to stalk the elusive Coues deer. Unaccompanied, Steve immerses himself in the quiet southwest desert and gets intimate with one of the most wary animals in the west. Throughout the hunt, Steve articulates why solo time in trophy country is good for the soul.

programme.title: MeatEater - Ribeye of the Sky: Sandhill Cranes in West Texas
programme.desc: Steve and bird hunting aficionado Ronny Boehme join up with wildlife biologist Ed Arnett in Lubbock, Texas.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Coldest Hunt: Nunivak Island Muskox
programme.desc: Lightning strikes twice: Steven Rinella has somehow beaten the odds and drawn a second Muskox tag after having to forfeit his first one four years ago due to unforeseen circumstances. Steve has always regretted passing up an opportunity to hunt Muskox but with another tag in his pocket, nothing will stop this adventure. We learn about the native history and culture of Nunivak Island's as Steve tries to stay warm while chasing Muskox 30 miles offshore in the Bering Sea.

programme.title: MeatEater - Gobbling: California Turkeys
programme.desc: The last time Joe Rogan and Bryan Callen went out with Steven Rinella they were soaked head to toe and came home meatless. To remedy their meat crisis while keeping them dry, Steve sets up a springtime wild turkey hunt in sunny California. Steve gives Joe and Bryan the A to Z on hunting, butchering, and cooking the wild turkey. Steve caps off the introductory lesson by preparing his favorite turkey recipe: Schnitzel.

programme.title: MeatEater - Unconventional: Alaska Sooty Grouse Part 1
programme.desc: In the first part of this series, Steve attempts a unique spring hunt for sooty grouse in the coastal rainforests of southeast Alaska. These mysterious hooters make for a hunt that is markedly different from any other and success doesn't come easily. They say blind squirrels can find a nut, but will Steve harvest a grouse?

programme.title: MeatEater - Unconventional: Alaska Sooty Grouse Part 2
programme.desc: In Part One, Steve experienced the difficulty of hunting the Sooty grouse of southeast Alaska.

programme.title: MeatEater - Wild to Table: Memorable Meals Cooking Special
programme.desc: Steve loves to fish as much as he loves to hunt.Steve is on a river in SW Montana for a day to catch.

programme.title: MeatEater - Up at the Cabin: Prince of Wales Island Black Bear
programme.desc: Last spring, Steve decided to pass up an opportunity to kill a black bear in favor of an extended up close and personal experience. This time, Steve heads back to his shack on Prince of Wales Island and he's brought his friend Paul Neess from Vortex Optics along to share the experience of using a canoe to slip in close to these giant black bears.

programme.title: MeatEater - Southern Traditions: Virginia Doves
programme.desc: All across America hunters kick off the fall hunting season with a weekend of doves, friends, and food. Steve's lifelong friend, Ronny Boehme has invited Steve out to his digs in Virginia for the dove opener. To prep for opening day, Steve and Ron spend a day brushing up on their shooting skills on a sporting clays course followed by a pigeon hunt. Steve shares simple, delicious cooking methods for doves and pigeons.

programme.title: MeatEater - Mountain Meditation: BC Grizzly Bear
programme.desc: Ryan Callaghan and Aaron Evans join Steve on an epic adventure in British Columbia's Rockies to pursue grizzly bears. Steve has long desired to take a grizzly but only if he can do it in a way that fits his personal ethics. The hunt is dampened by frustrating weather but Steve finds a way to fix a tasty meal.

programme.desc: Bear uses technology to monitor how he copes with the elements in Norway.

programme.desc: With startling revelations weighing in on him, Scott (Tyler Posey) finds himself scrambling to protect the people he loves, including Allison (Crystal Reed) who begins to follow clues left by her Aunt Kate about her family history.

programme.title: Formality
programme.desc: While struggling with family secrets, Allison (Crystal Reed) focuses on the Winter Formal dance. Meanwhile, as the tensions between werewolves and hunters rise to a boiling point, Scott (Tyler Posey) struggles to protect both friends and enemies.

programme.title: Code Breaker
programme.desc: In the season finale, Scott McCall (Tyler Posey) discovers one last clue which allows him to enlist both friends -- and even a previous enemy -- in order to stop the mysterious killings and win back Allison (Crystal Reed), the girl he loves.

programme.title: Omega
programme.desc: When Lydia (Holland Roden) goes missing from the hospital, werewolves and hunters alike begin a search to discover not only where she is, but what she may have become. Meanwhile Jackson's (Colton Haynes) body seems to be fighting against the bite.

programme.title: Shape Shifted
programme.desc: The night before a full moon, Isaac's father (John Wesley Shipp) is murdered. When Isaac (Daniel Sharman) is arrested, Scott (Tyler Posey), Stiles (Dylan O'Brien) and Derek (Tyler Hoechlin) resolve to break him out before the Argents get to him.

programme.title: Ice Pick
programme.desc: Scott (Tyler Posey) tries to stop Derek (Tyler Hoechlin) from creating new werewolves, while Chris Argent (JR Bourne) decides to officially begin Allison's (Crystal Reed) training. Meanwhile, the new creature in Beacon Hills takes another victim.

programme.title: Abomination
programme.desc: When the shapeshifter strikes again, Scott (Tyler Posey) has to recruit a reluctant Allison (Crystal Reed) to acquire a rare book from Gerard's (guest star Michael Hogan) collection, while also once again surviving dinner with the Argents.

programme.title: What a Day (The Real J6 Victims Are Standing Up)
programme.desc:
You know, President Donald Trump's 1.776 billion-dollar MAGA slush fund? The fund is intended for victims of "DOJ weaponization," like the January 6 insurrectionists Trump pardoned. Shockingly, very few people support it. And now former Capitol Police Officer Harry Dunn and a colleague, actual victims of the January 6 insurrection, are suing the Trump administration to put a stop to this fund. Jane speaks with Dunn about the slush fund, his lawsuit, and why it's so important to him to stand up for what actually happened that day.
And in headlines: Trump has yet another physical, Republicans' redistricting war takes a hit, and the Trump administration thinks it may be a good idea to have federal employees sign NDAs. Just in case...

programme.title: Keep It (The Colbert Necessities)
programme.desc: Louis Virtel is joined by Guy Branum to discuss the Canadian magic of Martin Short and his new documentary Marty, Life is Short. Then, Louis and Guy chat about The Late Show with Stephen Colbert ending and what that means for late night television. Plus, friend of the show Mandy Moore returns to chat pop music and her new movie The Breadwinner.

programme.title: Assembly Required with Stacey Abrams (How Anxious Should You Be About AI?)
programme.desc: AI development is barreling ahead with no guardrails, leading Americans to feel anxious about what lies ahead. This week, Stacey tackles the big questions we need to ask ourselves about the technology with Katie Drummond, Global Editorial Director at Wired, and Timnit Gebru, founder and Executive Director at the Distributed AI Research Institute (DAIR). They discuss the economic uncertainty created by AI, the motivations driving tech leaders, making sense of the flurry of information and opinions that are influencing the discourse on the issue, and whether we can imagine a more optimistic technologically-forward future.

programme.title: Pod Save the World (Pope Warns of AI Apocalypse)
programme.desc:
Tommy and Ben dig into a week of an “almost” deal, a sweeping AI warning, and a terrifying Ebola outbreak.
First up, the Iran deal that was supposedly days away somehow produced new US airstrikes instead, and the guys break down why the path to a permanent ceasefire remains littered with obstacles from Netanyahu wanting to escalate in Lebanon, to Trump suggesting Iran join the Abraham Accords. Then they bid a fond farewell to Tulsi Gabbard, who resigned as Director of National Intelligence under circumstances that depend entirely on who you ask. Pope Leo XIV dropped a 42,000-word encyclical on artificial intelligence that urges leaders to grapple with the impacts it will have on war, democracy, and the future of humanity. Trump’s Taiwan sellout is now official, with the administration quietly pausing a $14 billion arms sale just in case they need the munitions to fight Iran. The guys also give a sobering update on the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, and a man raises awareness for prostate cancer and bullying in schools by putting his manhood on the line. And finally, Tommy sits down with Ben about his brand-new book, All We Say: The Battle for American Identity: A History in 15 Speeches, out now.

programme.title: What a Day (The Real J6 Victims Are Standing Up)
programme.desc:
You know, President Donald Trump's 1.776 billion-dollar MAGA slush fund? The fund is intended for victims of "DOJ weaponization," like the January 6 insurrectionists Trump pardoned. Shockingly, very few people support it. And now former Capitol Police Officer Harry Dunn and a colleague, actual victims of the January 6 insurrection, are suing the Trump administration to put a stop to this fund. Jane speaks with Dunn about the slush fund, his lawsuit, and why it's so important to him to stand up for what actually happened that day.
And in headlines: Trump has yet another physical, Republicans' redistricting war takes a hit, and the Trump administration thinks it may be a good idea to have federal employees sign NDAs. Just in case...

programme.title: Keep It (The Colbert Necessities)
programme.desc: Louis Virtel is joined by Guy Branum to discuss the Canadian magic of Martin Short and his new documentary Marty, Life is Short. Then, Louis and Guy chat about The Late Show with Stephen Colbert ending and what that means for late night television. Plus, friend of the show Mandy Moore returns to chat pop music and her new movie The Breadwinner.

programme.title: A Husband For Eleanor
programme.desc: Lisa plays bovine matchmaker when Oliver and Lisa decide it is time for their female cow, Eleanor, to start providing milk for the farm. Oliver makes arrangements for Eleanor to mooooove in with the neighbor's bull, but Lisa has other ideas.

programme.title: Old Mail Day
programme.desc: The townsfolk look forward to "Old Mail Day", when once a year undelivered mail is handed out. The delayed mail includes a letter all the way from 1917! Oliver is upset by the poor service, but his complaints land with more impact than he intended.

programme.title: The Agricultural Student
programme.desc: When word spreads that an agricultural student is coming to Hooterville to study with county agent Hank Kimball, no one seems to be interested. However, things change when the folks discover that the student is an attractive blonde.

programme.title: How Hooterville Was Floundered
programme.desc: This episode tells the unusual tale of Hooterville's founding. As the town prepares for its centennial, Oliver announces a reenactment of the town's origins and casts himself and Lisa in the roles of Horace and Doris Hooter.

programme.title: The Blue Feather
programme.desc: When a blue feather arrives in the mail for Lisa, her Hungarian roots tell her that trouble is brewing. The feather is a symbol of malfortune to come, and she suspects it was sent by Hungarian gypsies, a warning that they are out to get her.

programme.title: Everywhere A Chick Chick
programme.desc: Don't count chickens before they hatch! Oliver thinks he's laid a gold egg of an idea when he decides to dive beak first into the business of raising chickens. Lisa, on the other hand, sees little difference between raising chickens and children.

programme.title: A Prize In Each And Every Package
programme.desc: All that glitters is not frosted cereal. After a jewelry heist in Chicago, the robbers foolishly stash their booty in the grain bin of a cereal manufacturer. Oliver and Lisa discover the jewels in their bowl of Crickly Wickly!

programme.title: Law Partners
programme.desc: Oliver Wendell Douglas returns from farm to firm... law firm, that is. When a young and ambitious local graduates from law school, he looks for a potential partner in Hooterville. Oliver is the logical choice. Lisa and Eb help the new venture.

programme.title: A Day In The Life Of Oliver Wendell Holmes
programme.desc: After partnering with a young, local lawyer, Oliver has opened a new law office in Hooterville. However, their secretary, Lisa, shows a lack of necessary skills. Soon, Lisa's job is on the line, even if she is married to one of the partners.

programme.title: Economy Flight To Washington
programme.desc: When the Young Peoples Agricultural Society holds their annual convention in Washington, D.C., local members Eb, Mr. Kimball, and Arnold the pig get ready to make the big trip. Oliver and Lisa serve as unintended chaperones.

programme.title: Retreat From Washington
programme.desc: While Lisa and Oliver consider what to do on their unplanned trip to Washington D.C., Mr. Haney rents their house to a family of eight. Meanwhile in the nation's capital, Lisa and Eb tour the White House, and Mr. Kimball makes official trouble.

programme.title: A Hunting We Won't Go
programme.desc: When a charmingly sweet doe wanders onto the Douglas farm, an enchanted Lisa befriends the beautiful animal. Having had her heart won over, Lisa resolves to have all deer hunting banned in Hooterville, winning support however necessary.

programme.title: Eb Uses His Ingenuity
programme.desc: Farmhand Eb plans to take his girlfriend to a fancy restaurant for her birthday. Only problem is he can't afford it. Oliver encourages Eb to get creative and figure out how to earn the money himself. And if Eb is anything, it's creative.

programme.title: The Old Trunk
programme.desc: Lisa becomes enchanted with the diary of a Lydia Plunkett. Found in an old trunk in the barn, the diary from 1898 tells Lydia's life story as a successful businesswoman. Eb and Lisa are glued to every page of the story, but Oliver isn't impressed.

programme.title: The Milk Maker
programme.desc: Mr. Haney and Fred Ziffel try to persuade Oliver to invest in a new business venture. "Looney Luke" Needlinger claim to have invented a machine that can turn hay into fresh milk. Oliver is skeptical and tries to prove the machine is a white lie.

programme.title: Lisa's Mudder Comes For A Visit
programme.desc: Lisa's "Mudder" pays a surprise visit to Lisa and Oliver. Eb good-naturedly introduces her as the Queen of Sicily. As a result, the entire town treats her like royalty. They even go so far as to hold a special day in her honor.

programme.title: Where There's A Will
programme.desc: Arnold Ziffel, Hooterville's beloved swine is thought to have rightful claim to a $20,000 fortune. The Birnbacher pork empire has promised that the descendant of the famous weather-predicting pig, Herman, will inherit the vast sum.

programme.title: The Wrong Stuff: January 24, 1961
programme.desc: As a NASA chimp, Sam's fate is dubious.

programme.title: Dreams: February 28, 1979
programme.desc: Al learns Sam is a murder target.

programme.desc: Rockford looks into death of P.I.

programme.title: Lurch's Little Helper
programme.desc: Morticia's concern that Lurch is overworked leads Gomez to the operating table to assemble a custom-built secondary butler. Lurch lets the robot do all the work and then begins to feel that he isn't needed anymore.

programme.title: The Addams Policy
programme.desc: When Uncle Fester incinerates the family's stuffed bear with his flamethrower, Morticia reports the accident to the insurance company. Mr. Henson, the head of the company, sends his assistant, Mr. Digby, to handle the case.

programme.title: Lurch's Grand Romance
programme.desc: Lurch suffers from unrequited love over Tiny Trivia (guest star Diane Jergens), Morticia's visiting school chum who only has eyes for show business. The entire family decides to try to turn their stolid butler in to a new personality.

programme.title: Ophelia's Career
programme.desc: When Ophelia laments that she has been jilted, Morticia suggests a career as an alternative to marriage. She tries chemistry first (with explosive results) then turns to operatic singing. Cousin Itt is summoned to coach her to stardom.

programme.title: The Addams Family Goes To School
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez decide to keep Wednesday and Pugsley home from school after Morticia sees the fairy tales they have to read. When the truant officer comes to investigate, he is shocked at what he finds.

programme.title: Morticia And The Psychiatrist
programme.desc: Morticia calls a psychiatrist after she fears Pugsley is becoming normal after he abandons his pet octopus for a puppy and is seen wearing a Boy Scout uniform, playing with a baseball bat, and helping an old lady cross the street.

programme.title: Fester's Punctured Romance
programme.desc: Uncle Fester, who has become lonely, convinces Gomez and Morticia that it is time for him to find a bride. He decides to put an ad in the newspaper, looking for love. But things go awry when a door-to-door saleswoman comes by.

programme.title: Gomez, The Politician
programme.desc: Election fever grips the Addams household, as Gomez (John Astin) supports the council candidate whose campaign promise is to drain the town's disgusting bogs. Morticia (Carolyn Jones), who likes "the lovely bogs," labels the candidate an extremist.

programme.title: The Addams Family Tree
programme.desc: Pugsley and Wednesday start a row at a party when their gift of a tarantula upsets Harold, the birthday boy. Harold's parents insist that the Addams children lack breeding and, therefore, are no longer allowed to play with their son.

programme.title: Morticia Joins The Ladies League
programme.desc: After Pugsley befriends Gorgo, a circus gorilla, Gorgo decides to follow Pugsley and Gomez home, where he is accepted as one of the family. But Gorgo gives Morticia's tea guests, the Ladies League members, quite a shock.

programme.title: Halloween With The Addams Family
programme.desc: A pair of bank robbers are welcomed as Halloween trick-or-treaters by Morticia and Gomez. The creepy atmosphere of the house, Morticia's smoldering holiday punch and Lurch's ominous presence impel the crooks to abandon their plans.

programme.title: Green Eyed Gomez
programme.desc: When Lionel Barker, a former suitor of Morticia, shows up, Gomez is jealous. But he's soon calmed when he learns that Barker is after Mildred, the new maid. The Addamses then discover that Barker is a con man--after Mildred's money, not her love.

programme.title: New Neighbors Meet The Addams Family
programme.desc: Newlyweds move in next door. But the honeymoon loses its tranquility when Uncle Fester enters their house through a tunnel and they flee in terror at the sight of Lurch, Grandmama sharpening her ax, and Morticia and Gomez bathing the octopus.

programme.title: Wednesday Leaves Home
programme.desc: The family faces a crisis when Wednesday packs her spider and runs away (after being scolded for playing with Uncle Fester's TNT). When they try to file a missing person report, a detective is anxious to retire early due to their story.

programme.title: The Addams Family Meets The V.I.P.'s
programme.desc: Official visitors from an unfriendly nation receive the shock of their lives when they decide to exploit the Addams Family as an average uncultured family. The visitors are shaken up by Morticia's carnivorous plants among other things.

programme.title: Morticia, The Matchmaker
programme.desc: Morticia's cousin, Melancholia, wants to marry Clarence P. Harvey after most of her marital prospects have joined the Foreign Legion. Clarence recoils when Melancholia puts on a campaign to win him, backed by Morticia, Gomez and Clarence's boss.

programme.title: Lurch Learns To Dance
programme.desc: Lurch becomes a veritable gazelle when he takes dancing lessons at Gomez and Morticia's suggestion, after he admits to being a wallflower at social functions. Each member of the Addams Family teaches Lurch a different step.

programme.title: The Other Side
programme.desc: Dr. Eberhardt (Ralph Macchio) discovers a way for comatose patients to communicate with each other by interfacing with a computer. But when he hooks himself up to the machine, he discovers a cyberspace "paradise" he may never be able to leave!

programme.title: Joyride
programme.desc: Cliff Robertson -- who starred in the very first episode of the classic series -- returns to the Outer Limits as an aging astronaut whose 1963 mission was a career-ending disaster. If he can just get back to space, perhaps he can clear his name.

programme.title: The Human Operators
programme.desc: Like other Star Fleet vessels, Ship (voice of Malcolm McDowell) has evolved and now controls the only human (Jack Noseworthy) aboard. But the relationship takes a dramatic turn when Man meets Woman and fights machine in a desperate bid for freedom!

programme.title: Blank Slate
programme.desc: Do our memories define our identity? If we are merely the sum of our experiences, what do we become if those experiences are lost? Hope Wilson (Robbie Chong) faces this question when she meets a man who has purposely erased his own memories.

programme.title: What Will The Neighbors Think?
programme.desc: Courtesy of a freak accident (and The Outer Limits), an agoraphobe (Jane Adams) begins to hear the private thoughts of all of the tenants in her building...the good, the bad and the steamy. And it seems at least one of them may want to kill her.

programme.title: The Shroud
programme.desc: A scientist -- who is also a religious zealot (David Ogden Stiers) -- secretly implants an embryo created with DNA from the Shroud of Turin into a young woman (Samantha Mathis) in order to scientifically engineer the Second Coming of Christ.

programme.title: Ripper
programme.desc: The Outer Limits come to Victorian Whitechapel when a London doctor (Cary Elwes) races to capture the alien creature responsible for the Jack the Ripper murders. But when the alien frames the good doctor for the murders, his time may be up.

programme.title: Tribunal
programme.desc: The son (Saul Rubinek) of a concentration camp survivor (Peter Boretski) gets some unique help in proving the guilt of a former Nazi (Jan Rubes) living in the U.S....courtesy of a time traveler (Alex Daikun) and, of course, The Outer Limits.

programme.title: Summit
programme.desc: The planets Earth and Dregocia struggle to re-establish a fragile trust after a summit goes tragically wrong. Can a diplomat (Marcia Cross) calm the hawkish nature of both species…and can she overcome her own prejudices to save both worlds?
